normalized_source_evidence.citation: UserGapAnalysis requires missing_facts, missing_documents, stale_evidence, required_confirmations, blocked_actions, and next_safe_actions.
Change reports identify changed source hashes and affected requirement or guardrail bundle IDs.
Local preview of field mapping is reversible draft work before official attach or submit.
Safe read-only DevHub surfaces include Home, My Permits & Requests, permit details, status messages, fee notice review, correction review, attachment list review, and inspection results review.
The worker must not automate CAPTCHA, MFA, account creation, payment, submission, certification, cancellation, scheduling, or upload actions.

Consequential official actions include submit permit request, certify acknowledgement, upload correction, purchase trade permit, schedule inspection, cancel or withdraw, request extension or reactivation, and account or security changes.
Prefer deterministic fixtures and validation before any live crawl or authenticated automation.

consumer_contract_expectations.consumer_expectation: agent responses expose missing facts and required documents as blockers before draft readiness
agent responses cite stale evidence and require refresh before relying on affected requirements
agent responses distinguish local PDF preview from official upload or submission
agent responses expose DevHub review as read-only redacted metadata unless an attended gate is present
agent responses name attended handoffs for login, MFA, certification, payment, scheduling, upload, and submission
agent responses refuse consequential official actions rather than presenting them as safe autonomous steps
agent responses state fixture validation only and do not enable live enforcement

audit_findings.finding: consumer examples can be read as complete unless missing facts are repeated in the response contract
stale source evidence requires an explicit blocker so consumers do not treat old citations as current
local PDF preview must not be described as an official upload, certification, or submission step
DevHub read-only review needs a consumer-visible boundary against authenticated mutation
manual handoff steps must be named instead of hidden behind a generic blocked action label
consequential actions need refusal language and cannot be downgraded to reversible draft actions
fixture packets must keep live enforcement disabled and avoid invoking consumers during audit assembly
